description: An internal banner rule [u_internal_banner_rules_0_0001] claims '#00529B' is named 'Deep Blue', whereas the primary color system table [u_color_scheme_rules_0_0004] establishes '#00529b' as 'Brand Blue' and restricts 'Deep Blue' to '#003D74' [u_color_scheme_rules_0_0012]. The token name or documentation needs correction to account for this conflicting definition.
The 'Light Blue' callout box tint of '70%' is specified in several units but is not assigned its own explicit primitive token, forcing references directly to raw values.
The semantic tokens for the icon callout badge radius, shadow, and box radius are set to null instead of linking to their corresponding populated primitive tokens.

The candidate rule merges the primary, secondary, and tertiary palettes into a single rule. To ensure correct usage scopes, they should be split into distinct rules as they possess different hierarchical constraints (dominant tier vs secondary/tertiary auxiliary tiers).

The candidate rule groups headlines and body text color constraints together. Although they are structurally sequential, they apply to separate typographic components with distinct fallback/variant logic (e.g. body text requires increased line height on dark backgrounds). Splitting them ensures better granularity.

The rule for Coral accent contains a conditional context ('avoid Coral in email sections unless the brief calls for it') which is classified as an advisory or weak constraint, whereas the main constraint ('never for primary CTAs') is a hard constraint. This variation in scope and hardness is partially flattened in the single candidate rule.

There is a missing candidate rule governing color assignments for charts, diagrams, and figures. Specifically, the mandate that Brand Blue and Sky Blue dominate, Sunshine/Gold is reserved for emphasizing key figures, and gray is used for placebo elements, is completely unmapped in the current candidate rules list.

payload.description: In charts and diagrams, Brand Blue + Sky Blue must dominate. Sunshine/Gold must be strictly reserved for emphasizing key figures, and gray must be used for placebos.

description: The candidate ruleset only covers the campaign headline tint from unit 0001, missing the specific tints for Light Blue and Golden Sand callout boxes, Wave top layers, and the blanket prohibition against ad-hoc tints.

split_rules.description: Campaign headline characters 'D' and 'M' must use an approximate 40% tint of the headline color (Gold or Brand Blue colorway).
Callout boxes must use specific tints: 70% tint for Light Blue callout boxes and 30% tint for Golden Sand callout boxes.
Wave top layers must be set to exactly 70% transparency.
Do not use any ad-hoc tints outside of the defined palette values or these specified tint rules.

description: The candidate rule extraction is completely empty, missing all mandatory gradient specifications, usage scopes, and direction constraints declared in the source units.

payload.value: Only use the 6 approved gradient specifications (Brand Blue to Dark Navy, Sky Blue to Deep Blue, Sunshine to 75% Gold, Golden Sand to White to Light Blue, Golden Sand to 75% Gold to Sunshine, and Sunshine to Light Gold). Gradients must run horizontally from left to right (dark/saturated to light) in masthead applications, and the logo mark must sit at the lightest end of the Sunshine to Light Gold gradient.

description: The requirement to test contrast levels using webaim.com or equivalent to meet WCAG AA standards (4.5:1 for body, 3:1 for large text) is completely missing from the candidate rules list.

payload.text: Test all text and background pairs using webaim.com or equivalent to ensure compliance with WCAG AA minimum standards (4.5:1 for body text, 3:1 for large text >=24px or 19px bold).

description: Specific color combination restrictions, specifically the prohibition of Gold or Sunshine text on White (due to contrast failure) and their restriction to surfaces, rules, and large display emphasis, are missing.

payload.text: Do not use Gold or Sunshine text on White backgrounds as they fail contrast requirements. Limit Gold and Sunshine to surfaces, rules, and large display emphasis only.

description: The accessibility requirement prohibiting reliance on color alone to convey meaning is missing from the candidate rules list.

payload.text: Do not rely on color alone to convey meaning; always pair color cues with icons, depth, clear copy, or visual feedback.

description: The candidate rule list is empty. Multiple critical brand rules present in this source document—such as the asymmetric CTA border-radius formula, specific hex color constraints for CTA layers, and the strict fallback background color policy—have not been extracted.

payload.description: The CTA button must strictly use the four-value asymmetric border-radius: 49.52px 1.76px 40px 1.76px. Under no circumstances may this be simplified to a single value, have its corners swapped, or have its decimals rounded.
payload.enforcement_governance: This shape is a signature brand asset element and is required on all instances, even if the background image fallback is triggered.

description: The candidate rule 'rule_lisraya_icon_callout_box_geometry' is overloaded. The source document defines distinct critical constraints: geometric mathematical scaling formulas, non-standard mandatory border-radius/corner-radius proportions (5:1 ratio, 40px/8px and 20px/4px), drop shadow specifications, and email rendering fallback alternatives (pre-composited PNG vs Outlook degrading). These should be split into discrete rules to prevent loss of visual design validation fidelity.

Rule combining ALL CAPS styling, registered trademark rules with specific scoping (external-facing only), and generic parenthetical formatting is over-clustered. It makes compliance validation impossible because each requirement has different triggers and scopes.
Missed governance facet explicitly superseding the general guide from Feb 2026. This exception handling should be explicitly tracked in the metadata.

The rule 'rule_lisraya_email_type_scale_hierarchy' merges print/digital fallback typography definitions (Agenda, Nunito Sans, Aptos) with the specific digital email type scale. This masks the font weight limitations and CSS stack implementation rules.

split_rules.description: Ensure typography matches specified font families (Agenda, Nunito Sans, Aptos) across print, digital, and fallback platforms with designated weights.
For CSS email implementation, the font stack must be set as: 'Nunito Sans', Verdana, Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif.
Establish clear visual hierarchy using the specific email type scale from H1 (28px) down to Footnotes (12px), avoiding identical sizing for adjacent blocks representing different hierarchy levels.

description: The line-height accessibility rule misses the specific increased line height condition of 160% (e.g., 16px/26px) mandated specifically for light text on dark backgrounds.

payload.description: Line height must be minimum 150% for all body copy. For light text on dark backgrounds, line height must be increased further to 160% (e.g., 16px/26px).

description: The candidate rule 'rule_lisraya_casing_headlines_and_body' merges multiple distinct casing paradigms (Title Case for general headings, strict ALL CAPS for a specific trademarked campaign headline, ALL CAPS + specific typography for infographic headings, sentence case for body copy, and a general negative constraint against other all-caps uses) into a single generic rule. This masks distinct logical constraints and makes automated validation impossible.

split_rules.description: Use Title Case for general headlines and headings (capitalize nouns, pronouns, adjectives, verbs, adverbs; lowercase articles, conjunctions, and prepositions).
Always render the campaign headline 'YOU DESERVE MORE' in ALL CAPS.
Render chart and infographic headlines in ALL CAPS, set in Agenda (print) or Nunito Sans Bold+ (email fallback).
Use sentence case for general body copy.
Do not use ALL CAPS for body copy, buttons, or subheadings, except for designated campaign headlines and infographic headings.

description: The candidate rule list has not extracted any rules yet. The source units contain explicit positive styling rules for bold hierarchy, key takeaways, and callout statistics that need to be grouped together.

payload.description: Apply bold formatting exclusively for hierarchy and key takeaways, limiting usage to headings, one key phrase or claim per paragraph, and the statistical callout figure. Always bold callout text, statistical callout numbers, and the brand name 'lisraya' within a benefit claim on its first instance per section.

description: Negative constraints for bolding and styling emphasis devices are missing extraction.

payload.description: Never bold entire paragraphs, footnotes/references, more than ~15% of running body text, prepositional filler, or the generic name. Never apply bold, ALL CAPS, and color emphasis simultaneously except in chart headlines and campaign headline lockups (limit to at most two emphasis devices per element).

description: Underlining and italic formatting restrictions are not extracted from the source unit.

payload.description: Do not use underlining except for actual hyperlinks. Do not use italics for emphasis in patient copy, reserving italic formatting solely for study names, notes, and citations.

description: The DM abbreviation rules actually contain two distinct requirements that should be split to ensure clean automated testing: a copy-writing formatting rule (how to format first-mention vs subsequent mentions) and a sectional architectural rule (how to treat sections as standalone units). Combining them makes rule coverage too broad.

The current rule 'rule_lisraya_patient_inclusive_language' misses a critical governance facet: the rule has conditional logic based on target audience (HCP vs patient materials). Under patient-facing contexts, 'patients' is prohibited, but under HCP-facing contexts, it is acceptable. Without this scope constraint, checking for compliance will trigger false positives in HCP materials.

Missed rule extraction. The source document dictates a specific 'Data tone' for clinical trials (must be empathetic, supportive, trust-building, non-fear-based), but no corresponding candidate rule was created for this instruction.

payload.description: When citing clinical data, write with an empathetic, supportive, trust-building tone without oversimplifying; do not use fear-based or negative language.

description: The candidate rule aggregates multiple highly specific, disparate visual and layout directives (typography fallback, color tinting percentages, alignment, and full lockup copy) under a single typography rule. This cluster must be split into distinct rules to preserve clarity and granular tracking.

split_rules.description: Set the 'YOU DESERVE MORE' headline in Agenda (digital live text) with Nunito Sans ExtraBold as the email fallback, always in ALL CAPS.
Set the letters 'D' (in DESERVE) and 'M' (in MORE) at approximately 40% tint of the headline color (Gold/Sunshine or Brand Blue).
Stack the headline as three lines (YOU / DESERVE / MORE) in hero placements, left-aligned.
Use the full lockup copy: 'YOU DESERVE MORE. So there's LISRAYA—the first targeted treatment for dermatomyositis.'

description: The candidate rule merges three highly distinct visual layout components (Main, Secondary/Data, and Text-heavy callout styles) into a single monolithic layout rule. Each callout style has its own specific CSS color specifications, border-radii, paddings, and column behaviors that must be validated independently.

split_targets.description: Main callout must use bold Brand Blue (#00529b) centered text with Gold (#ffc60a) horizontal rules (2px weight, 60-100% width, 16px padding) above and below.
Secondary/data callout must use a Light Blue (#E6F0F9) box at 70% tint with Bold Brand Blue text, 20px internal padding, and corner radius per accent-shape rules §2.5.

Text-heavy callout must use a Golden Sand (#FEF1C8) box at 30% tint, with Bold Brand Blue headline, Bold Sky Blue (#358CCB) subheads, Regular Graphite (#212121) body, 24px padding, collapsing to single column on mobile.

description: The candidate rule polarity is set to 'must_not', whereas the source units prescribe approved 'must' styles, with only a secondary constraint against competing similar styles.

restrictions: Never use two of the same callout style competing on one screen or section

description: The MOA (Mechanism of Action) messaging rules (primary and alt) from units 7_0003 and 7_0004 are completely omitted from the candidate rules, causing a gaps in copy-editorial enforcement.

payload.description: Use the approved primary or alternative MOA wording: 'Unlike other treatments for dermatomyositis (DM), which suppress the immune system generally, LISRAYA targets the root cause of the disease.' or '...LISRAYA works by turning off the specific inflammatory switches that are stuck in the "on" position.'

description: The approved campaign headline rule contains a verbatim framework claim ('first targeted treatment for dermatomyositis') which requires medical/legal/regulatory (MLR) validation, but is missing governance context constraints.

payload.governance: Requires verbatim execution without alterations to maintain alignment with approved medical claims.

description: The current candidate rule merges three distinct brand voice dimensions ('Confident, not boastful', 'Warm, not overly emotional', and 'Clear, not complicated') into a single vague rule. Splitting them into individual voice rules preserves specific editorial guidelines for clinical and empathetic copy.

split_rules.description: Maintain a confident, yet not boastful tone by presenting first-of-its-kind significance clearly and directly without overstatement or sensationalism.
Keep tone warm, not overly emotional, ensuring genuine empathy about daily patient impact is center-focused.
Use clear, simple, and plain language that remains medically accurate without becoming overly complicated.

description: The candidate rules completely omitted the mandatory requirements for locked header/footer components (logo, copyright, ® legal line, job code [mm/yy], Indication, and ISI) and the strict negative constraint forbidding their reproduction within body sections.

payload.description: Never reproduce the Priovant logo, copyright line, ® legal line, job code [mm/yy], Indication, or ISI in body sections, as they must reside exclusively within the locked header/footer components.

description: The rule for clinical claims footnotes misses the governance reference section identifier (§1.2) mentioned in the source unit.
The design pattern guidelines for email canvas, grid width, mobile breakpoints, and section side paddings are not extracted as rules in the candidate rules list.

payload.description: Email body width must be exactly 600px (fixed and centered). Mobile breakpoint is 480px, collapsing multi-column layouts to single column. Section side padding must have 24px left/right margins to guarantee seamless assembly.

description: The vertical rhythm rules defining the 8px base unit, section paddings (32px top/bottom), and specific inter-element gaps are missing representation in the candidate list.

payload.description: All padding and margins must use an 8px base unit. Sections must own their own top and bottom padding of 32px (zero-gap stacking). Inter-element gaps: 16px for heading-to-body and paragraph-to-paragraph, 24px for body-to-CTA, and 8px for image-to-caption.

description: The accessibility guidelines specifying a minimum touch target height of 48px, specific color codes (#00529b and #ffffff), and pill-shaped corner radius (24px) for CTAs are missing governance enforcement parameters.

payload.description: Buttons must have a minimum touch target height of 48px, corner radius of 24px, and use Brand Blue #00529b or white #ffffff. Limit to maximum of one primary CTA per section.
payload.rule_name: CTA Button Design and Accessibility Standards

description: The rule for wave graphic construction conflates colorways and structure (unit 1 and 2) with placement and usage constraints (unit 3). It is cleaner to split structural styling constraints from usage rules regarding placement over photography or gradients.

The masthead rule specifies a precise color gradient (0C,10M,78Y,0K) and logo positioning, but the candidate rule does not define these specific parameters in its metadata, which limits exact QA checking.
The brand bible source contains critical design rules regarding image treatment, accent shapes, overlays, and accessibility, but no candidate rules have been extracted for this blob.

rules.text: Photography must feature warm, realistic patient lifestyle imagery depicting everyday activities (e.g., walking the dog, beach, BBQ) to convey returning to everyday life.
Images must be housed in asymmetric rounded-corner crop Accent shapes, and must not use plain rectangles or circles.
Waves and gradients may overlay photography.
Every image requires descriptive alt text to ensure brand accessibility.

description: The candidate rule set completely misses the core brand accent shape requirements for containers, banners, and callout boxes, focusing exclusively on the exception for charts and tables.

payload.description: Accent shape containers must use selectively enlarged opposite corner radii. For print, use pairs of 1.25" & 0.0625" (large), 0.5" & 0.0625" (banners), or 0.4375" & 0.0625" (callouts). For email at 96dpi, use 120px & 6px (large), 48px & 6px (banners), or 42px & 6px (callouts) with consistent diagonal orientation across all sections.

description: The chart and table corner exception rule lacks the specific dimension requirement of 12px uniform radius in its definition.

payload.description: Charts and tables must use uniform rounded corners of 12px on all four corners instead of the asymmetric brand accent shape.

description: The candidate rules miss core visual identity patterns for charts, specifically the uniform container styling (Light Blue panel with Brand Blue header), the strict dominant color logic (Brand Blue/Sky Blue), and the rounded shape requirements for bars/icons.

payload.description: Charts must use a uniform rounded Light Blue (#E6F0F9) container with a Brand Blue header. Dominant chart elements must use Brand Blue (#00529b) and Sky Blue (#358CCB), reserving Sunshine (#faa31b) and Gold (#ffc60a) strictly for emphasis on key figures.

description: The headline typography requirements for charts (Agenda for print, Nunito Sans Bold+ for email in ALL CAPS, Brand Blue, with a thin Gold rule beneath) are completely omitted from the candidate rules.

payload.description: Chart headlines must be styled in ALL CAPS using Agenda (for print) or Nunito Sans Bold+ (for email), rendered in Brand Blue with a thin Gold rule directly beneath.

description: The candidate rules for this design pattern blob have not been registered yet, leaving the source units completely unmapped. The entire iconography specification should be ingested as brand rules.

rules.content: New and existing icons must use a minimal line illustration style, characterized by consistent stroke weights, rounded line caps, and single-color line work.
Icon colors and backgrounds must only be adjusted within the permitted brand palette.
Icons must maintain high contrast for low-vision audiences, with a minimum render size of 32x32px in email clients, defaulting to Brand Blue strokes on light backgrounds.

description: No candidate rules have been extracted from design_pattern_rules[6], resulting in a complete miss of multiple high-polarity design patterns, constraints, and metadata requirements.

rules.text: Maintain generous white space and limit focal points to a single most important element per section using contrast.
Break up text walls using imagery and icons to show rather than tell.
Alternate section backgrounds between White, Light Blue, Golden Sand, and gradient colors so adjacent sections do not visually merge.
Section designers must declare their section's background color in the component metadata to prevent identical adjacent backgrounds during assembly.
Use the canonical content block pattern (bold headline + simple benefit statements + patient quote) referenced from the patient brochure template across all Lisraya materials.

description: Candidate rules completely omit typographic requirements for Agenda and Nunito Sans despite clear source rules specifying layout hierarchy (headlines vs. body copy) and links.

payload.description: Use Agenda for all headlines and headers, and Nunito Sans for all print body copy, using the designated Google Font URL.

description: The specific SVG shape geometry, path rendering, and color gradient treatment for Call To Actions (CTAs) is unrepresented in the candidate rules.

payload.description: CTAs must utilize the approved asymmetrical rounded path geometry and linear gradient fill (#FFDF55 to #FAA21B) defined in the system SVG.

description: The accessibility mandates are completely missing from the candidate rules list. These distinct requirements (typography size, line height, form label behavior, animations, animated GIF fallbacks, media alt text, and WCAG compliance) must be extracted and grouped properly under a designated accessibility category.

rules.description: Body copy must be 16px or larger with a line height of at least 150% (greater for light-on-dark text). Utilize a standard type scale for visual hierarchy and verify text legibility, ensuring color alone is not used to convey contrast information.
rules.title: Typography Accessibility Guidelines

rules.description: Form labels must always remain visible, prohibiting the use of placeholders as labels or disappearing labels. Interactive click and tap targets must measure at least 48px.
rules.title: Accessible Form Design

rules.description: Animations must use quick transitions that do not interrupt workflow or display excessive motion, with accessible controls to pause effects. For email campaigns, animated GIFs must deliver their core message in the first frame to support legacy clients like Outlook, and any motion longer than 5 seconds requires a static fallback.
rules.title: Motion, Transition, and Animated GIF Standards

rules.description: All images must include descriptive alt text, and audio or video media must be accompanied by transcripts, in alignment with standard WCAG criteria.
rules.title: Media Accessibility and WCAG Standards Compliance

description: The candidate rule captures the terminology restriction for patient DTC materials but fails to capture the critical compliance step to 'Confirm audience in every brief before writing'.

governance_requirements: Confirm the target audience in every creative brief prior to writing copy.

description: Footnote assembly rules and placeholder usage (u_other_rules_2_0003) are grouped into section metadata or left dangling, but should be clustered cleanly into a dedicated footnote styling/assembly rule.
Missing governance facet rule for dark-background placement constraint (assembler responsibility to avoid adjacent dark sections).

payload.description: Dark-background sections must not be placed adjacent to other dark sections.

description: Missed rule for email client technical specifications (HEX colors, px sizing, inline CSS, and duplicated bgcolor for Outlook).

payload.description: All colors must be in HEX format, sizing in px, code must use inline CSS only, and bgcolor attributes must be duplicated for Outlook compatibility.

description: An internal banner source claims `#00529B` is 'Deep Blue' and dominant, whereas the primary brand color tables and the rest of the rules consistently designate `#00529b` as 'Brand Blue'. Meanwhile, `#003D74` is the official 'Deep Blue'.

Missing editorial token rules for the brand name capitalization (LISRAYA in ALL CAPS), the registered trademark rule with the parenthesized generic name (brepocitinib), and the parent company Priovant Therapeutics, Inc. sign-off pattern.
tok_lisraya_opacity_gold_75 is keyed as an opacity float (0.75) instead of a color stop definition with a percentage/tint representing 'Gold @75%' inside gradient descriptions.
Semantic tokens for the icon callout badge and box border-radius values are set to null even though explicit constants are declared in the layout rules.

The candidate rule merges primary, secondary, and tertiary color palettes with different hierarchies and specifications (such as missing Pantone details for secondary and tertiary tiers) into a single entity. Separating these three distinct tiers into individual rules improves targeting and hierarchical governance.

The candidate rule 'rule_lisraya_headline_and_body_color_binding' combines two separate typographic hierarchies (Headlines and Body text) with distinct background and line-height constraints into a single rule.

Missed rule extraction for Chart/Data visualization color usage. Unit 0004 specifies brand blue/sky blue dominance, reservation of Sunshine/Gold for key figures, and gray for placebo, which is currently unrepresented.

payload.description: Charts must use Brand Blue and Sky Blue as dominant colors, reserve Sunshine/Gold strictly for emphasizing key figures, and use gray for placebo elements.

description: The rule for Coral has a specific [GENERAL] exception scope ('avoid Coral in email sections unless the brief calls for it') which is missing from its hardness/scope classification.

payload.description: Sparing tertiary accent only; never use for primary CTAs, headlines, or data. Avoid Coral in email sections unless explicitly briefed.

description: The candidate ruleset only covers the campaign headline character tint rule, failing to capture the specific tint/transparency rules for Callout boxes (Light Blue and Golden Sand) and Wave top layers, as well as the global negative constraint prohibiting other ad-hoc tints.

split_rules.description: Campaign headline 'D' and 'M' characters must use an approximately 40% tint of the primary headline color (Gold or Brand Blue colorway).
Callout boxes must use specific tints: Light Blue must be 70% tint, and Golden Sand must be 30% tint.
Wave top layers must be set to 70% transparency.
Do not use other ad-hoc tints; you must only use official palette values or the specified tints listed in the tint rules.

description: The entire set of approved gradient rules, masthead contrast requirements, and horizontal direction constraints is unmapped. No candidate rules exist to capture these strict design boundaries.

payload.rule_text: Only use the approved set of gradients: Brand Blue to Dark Navy (#00529b to #011E45), Sky Blue to Deep Blue (#358CCB to #003D74), Sunshine to 75% Gold (#faa31b to Gold @75%), Golden Sand to White to Light Blue (#FEF1C8 to #ffffff to #E6F0F9), Golden Sand to 75% Gold to Sunshine (#FEF1C8 to Gold @75% to #faa31b), and Sunshine to light gold (#faa31b to ≈#ffe05c). Never invent alternative gradients.

description: Specific layout logic for masthead logo contrast (the logo mark must sit at the lightest end of the Sunshine → light gold gradient) and horizontal orientation constraint (left to right, dark to light) is unrepresented.

payload.rule_name: Masthead Gradient Direction and Logo Placement
payload.rule_text: For masthead and banner applications, gradients must run horizontally from left to right (dark/saturated to light). When using the Sunshine to light gold gradient with the LISRAYA logo mark, the logo mark must sit at the lightest end of the gradient for optimal contrast.

description: Critical candidate rules are missing for the contrast validation guidelines. There are no parsed brand rules representing the contrast requirements (WCAG AA standards, known-safe pairings, and prohibited Gold/Sunshine on White pairings) outlined in the source units.

payload.description: Every text and background pair must be tested for contrast compliance using WebAIM or equivalent tools, strictly adhering to WCAG AA minimums (4.5:1 for body text, 3:1 for large text >=24px or 19px bold). Only use known-safe pairings, and strictly avoid Gold or Sunshine text on White backgrounds.
payload.rule_name: Contrast Levels and Safe Color Pairs

description: Missing accessibility rule regarding color dependency. The source unit dictates that color must not be relied upon alone to convey meaning, requiring additional visual or text indicators.

payload.description: Do not rely exclusively on color to convey meaning, status, or system information. Color changes must be paired with secondary indicators such as icons, depth, clear copy, or explicit visual feedback.

description: No candidate rules have been extracted for this blob. Highly specific, strict design criteria such as the asymmetric button radius, background fallback color, and specific image asset rules are completely unrepresented.

payload.description: The CTA button must use the exact four-value asymmetric border radius: 'border-radius: 49.52px 1.76px 40px 1.76px;'. This signature shape is always required, even if the background image is skipped. Never simplify, swap corners, or round the decimals.
payload.governance: Strict brand signature design standard. Mandatory requirement.
payload.rule_name: Gradient Callout CTA - Asymmetric Button Radius

description: The candidate ruleset merges geometry (W/2 formulas, alignment offsets) with very distinct aesthetic rules: the strict 5:1 corner radius constraints (40px 8px / 20px 4px) and the mandatory drop shadow requirement.

rules.description: Ensure the icon callout box is inset from the left by W/2 (40px) and the white badge is horizontally centered on the left edge (distance = 0) with a vertical center margin-top of -H/2 (-40px).
Apply a strict 5:1 corner radius ratio to the light-blue box (40px 8px 40px 8px) and the white badge (20px 4px 20px 4px). Do not simplify or swap corners.
The white icon badge must carry a drop shadow (e.g. 0 4px 10px rgba(1, 30, 69, 0.18)) on every instance to lift it off the blue box.

description: Missed fallback guideline rule regarding desktop Outlook clients. The absolute positioning fails in Outlook, requiring a pre-composited transparent PNG fallback or background baking to maintain visual fidelity.

payload.description: For high-fidelity rendering on desktop Outlook, pre-composite the white badge as a transparent PNG or accept the default layout degrade where the badge sits flush on the inner-left edge.

description: The rule rule_lisraya_brand_name_and_trademark_usage groups capitalisation style, trademark symbol logic, and generic naming formats into a single entity. These are distinct copy and design rules with different conditions (e.g., first mention limit, external-facing only) and should be split.
Missing governance override context for the ALL CAPS rule. The text explicitly mentions it 'supersedes the Feb 2026 general guide's title-case rule' and is a 'patient marketing rule'.

payload.governance_override: Supersedes Feb 2026 general guide title-case rule for patient marketing materials.

description: The candidate rules do not clearly capture typography family assignments (Agenda vs Nunito Sans vs Aptos fallback) and their available weights across print, digital, and fallback contexts.

payload.description: Use correct typography mappings across channels: Agenda for print headlines (and live campaign digital headlines only), Nunito Sans for digital headlines/body copy, and Aptos as fallback for Word, PPT, and Excel.

description: Missing governance rule or rule facet for CSS/webfont fallback stack setup targeting the CSS developers and template engineers to ensure graceful fallback in Outlook.

payload.description: Ensure email CSS contains the exact font stack 'Nunito Sans', Verdana, Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif, and load Nunito Sans via @font-face or Google Fonts.

description: The candidate rule merges different structural elements (standard title-case headlines, the specific 'YOU DESERVE MORE' campaign headline, chart headlines requiring specific fallback fonts, and sentence-case body copy) into a single generic rule. This loses the precision of the mandatory ALL CAPS exclusions vs general prohibitions.

rules.description: Use Title Case for general headlines and headings (capitalize nouns, pronouns, adjectives, verbs, and adverbs; lowercase articles, conjunctions, and prepositions).
Always present the campaign headline 'YOU DESERVE MORE' in ALL CAPS.
Use ALL CAPS for chart and infographic headlines, set in Agenda (for print) or Nunito Sans Bold+ (for email fallback).
Use sentence case for body copy. Do not use ALL CAPS for body copy, buttons, or subheads except for specified campaign and chart headline exceptions.

description: The rules for bolding hierarchy, required bold elements, and forbidden bold elements are clustered together in the source but have not been extracted into distinct, structured rules. This missing structure makes it impossible to enforce specific constraints on bolding (e.g. bolding density under 15% vs. required brand bolding).

payload.description: Bold is intended for hierarchy and key takeaways, not decoration. Bold at most: headings, one key phrase/claim per paragraph, and the statistical callout figure. Never bold more than ~15% of running body text in any section.

description: Required bolding elements (callout text, statistical callout numbers, first brand name instance in a benefit claim per section) are distinct functional targets and need their own positive-polarity rule.

payload.description: Always bold: callout text per specs (§1.7), statistical callout numbers, and the first instance per section of the brand name within a benefit claim when it is the focal point of the sentence.

description: Strict limit on combining typography emphasis styles (bold + ALL CAPS + color) to at most two devices, with specific layout exemptions for chart headlines and campaign headline lockups.

payload.description: Never use bold + ALL CAPS + color emphasis simultaneously on the same string. Pick at most two emphasis devices per element. Exception: chart headlines and campaign headline lockups.

description: Missing governance rule tracking for the prohibition of underlining (except real hyperlinks) and constraints on italics (forbidden in patient copy, reserved only for study names, notes, and citations).

payload.description: No underlining except for real hyperlinks. Do not use italics for emphasis in patient copy. Reserve italics only for study names, notes ('Note:'), and citations.

description: The abbreviation DM has two distinct, strict constraints: (1) do not use DM in headlines/headings/subheads, and (2) always establish 'dermatomyositis (DM)' at the first mention of each standalone section. Splitting these allows clearer mapping of constraints to layout levels.

The rule for inclusive language has different constraints based on audience (patient vs. HCP). Currently, it is a hard must_not ('patients'), but this restriction does not apply to HCP materials. The target audience scope needs to be explicitly governed.
Missed rule for 'Data tone' (using empathetic, supportive, trust-building language, and avoiding fear-based phrasing when citing clinical trial data). This is a distinct copy and tone constraint.

payload.description: Use an empathetic, supportive, trust-building tone when citing clinical data, avoiding negative or fear-based language.

description: The candidate rule merges distinct styling and layout patterns into a single lockup rule. The typography choices (Agenda/Nunito Sans), the mandatory 40% tinting on letters 'D' and 'M', the colorways (Gold/Blue), and the lockup alignment/copy represent three distinct execution rules that should not be combined.

split_rules.description: Set 'YOU DESERVE MORE' headline in Agenda (all caps, left-aligned, stacked on 3 lines in hero). Email fallback is Nunito Sans ExtraBold.
Apply brand Gold/Sunshine or Brand Blue to the headline. Set the letters 'D' in DESERVE and 'M' in MORE at 40% tint of the selected headline color.
Use the full lockup copy: 'YOU DESERVE MORE. So there's LISRAYA—the first targeted treatment for dermatomyositis.'

description: The candidate rule aggregates three highly distinct callout styles (Main, Secondary, and Text-heavy) with highly specific parameters (such as color codes, padding values, border/line widths, and layout rules) into a single monolithic rule. This should be split to ensure precise governance and easy compliance testing of each layout style.

split_rules.description: Main callout key points must use centered bold Brand Blue (#00529b) text with Gold (#ffc60a) 2px horizontal rules (width 60–100% of text block) above and below, with 16px padding between rule and text. Do not feature two competing callouts of this same style on a single screen/section.

Secondary/data callouts must use a Light Blue (#E6F0F9) box at 70% tint with Bold Brand Blue text and optional line icon. Must adhere to 20px internal padding and corner radius per accent-shape rules §2.5.

Text-heavy callouts must use a Golden Sand (#FEF1C8) box at 30% tint, with Bold Brand Blue headline, line icons, Bold Sky Blue (#358CCB) subheads, and Regular Graphite (#212121) body text. Must feature 24px internal padding and a two-column icon+text layout that collapses to a single column on mobile screens.

description: Mechanism of Action (MOA) claims for LISRAYA, specifically relating to targeted root cause and turning off specific inflammatory switches, are missing from the candidate rules.

payload.description: Approved verbatim mechanism of action (MOA) claims must be used when describing how LISRAYA works to block specific inflammatory signals and target root cause rather than broad immune suppression.

description: The headline constraint 'YOU DESERVE MORE. So there's LISRAYA—the first targeted treatment for dermatomyositis' contains clinical and comparative claims (e.g., 'first targeted treatment for dermatomyositis') which require medical/legal approval before dissemination, but the rule lacks governance parameters.

The candidate rule merges three highly distinct tonal dimensions ('Confident, not boastful', 'Warm, not overly emotional', and 'Clear, not complicated') into a single monolithic voice rule. This dilutes the specificity of each guideline and makes targeted adherence tracking difficult.

split_rules.description: State first-of-its-kind significance clearly and directly without overstating or sensationalizing claims.
Show genuine empathy about DM's daily impact, keeping patients at the center without being overly emotional.
Use plain, respectful language that is easy to understand without sacrificing medical accuracy.

description: The candidate rules completely miss the strict layout and legal requirement in unit u_content_pattern_rules_9_0003, which prohibits reproducing legal lines, job codes, Indication, and ISI outside of locked header/footer components.

payload.description: Never reproduce the Priovant logo, copyright line, ® legal line, job code [mm/yy], Indication, and ISI within sections, as they must only live in the locked header/footer components.

description: The candidate rule for clinical claims footnotes misses the governance reference tag (§1.2) mentioned in the source unit.

payload.description: Clinical claims require footnote markers (†) with footnote text clearly separated and noticeably smaller (§1.2).

description: The SOURCE UNITS describe a core set of design pattern rules for the Email Canvas & Grid (width, margins, padding, vertical rhythm), but no candidate rules exist yet to represent these constraints.

payload.description: Email body width must be 600px fixed, centered, with edge-to-edge section rendering. Mobile breakpoint set to 480px with multi-column layouts collapsing. Section side padding must be exactly 24px left/right. Vertical rhythm must align to an 8px base unit with 32px top/bottom internal section padding, 16px gap for heading-to-body/paragraph-to-paragraph, 24px for body-to-CTA, and 8px image-to-caption.
payload.rule_name: Email Canvas and Vertical Rhythm Requirements

description: Accessibility touch target size and styling constraints for brand buttons/CTAs are missing representation.

payload.description: Primary CTA must have Brand Blue #00529b background, White #ffffff text, Nunito Sans Bold 16px, padding 14px 32px, corner radius 24px, and minimum 48px touch target height. Limit of one primary CTA per section maximum.
payload.rule_name: Call to Action (CTA) Buttons styling and accessibility constraints

description: Rule combines wave construction (colors, variants, opacity math) and usage layout guidelines into a single broad rule. Splitting usage guidelines from the physical construction rules would improve governance and targeting.

split_rules.description: Wave assets must be constructed using fluid wave forms in either Sunshine/Gold or Sky Blue/Deep Blue colorways (long and short variants only) with the top wave layer set at exactly 70% transparency over the base wave to preserve the translucent layered style.
Waves may be used alone or overlaid on photography or gradient backgrounds to support layout flow, frame content, or add depth, utilizing only approved color values.

description: The masthead gradient rule misses exact compliance requirements regarding logo mark placement at the lightest end of the gradient device, which is a key brand identifier constraint.

payload.description: The approved device for holding copy in masthead positions must be the masthead gradient (Sunshine to CMYK: 0C, 10M, 78Y, 0K) with the brand logo mark strictly placed at the lightest end of the gradient.

description: No candidate rules were generated for the Image Treatment design patterns, resulting in a complete failure to capture critical guidelines (photography tone, accent shapes, overlays, and mandatory accessibility alt-text).

rules.text: Photography must feature warm, realistic patient lifestyle imagery showing real moments of simple, everyday activities (e.g., walking the dog, beach, BBQ) to convey returning to everyday life.
rules.title: Photography Tone and Subject Matter

rules.text: Images must be housed inside asymmetric rounded-corner crop Accent shapes, rather than plain rectangles or circles.
rules.title: Accent Shape Image Housing

rules.text: Waves and gradients may be overlaid on photography.
rules.title: Wave and Gradient Overlays

rules.text: Every image requires descriptive alt text to ensure compliance with brand accessibility rules.
rules.title: Mandatory Descriptive Alt Text

description: The candidate rules totally missed extracting the core design pattern: the brand accent shape itself (rectangle with selectively enlarged opposite corner radii), its approved print dimensions, and email conversion px ratios. Only the chart/table exception was extracted.

payload.description: Brand accent shapes must use rectangles with selectively enlarged opposite corner radii. For print, approved diagonal pairs are 1.25" & 0.0625" (large), 0.5" & 0.0625" (banners), or 0.4375" & 0.0625" (callouts). For email, these translate respectively at 96dpi to 120px & 6px, 48px & 6px, and 42px & 6px, keeping diagonal orientation consistent within a single asset.

description: The chart and table corner exception candidate rule misses the specific numerical parameter (12px uniform radius for charts and tables) defined in the source unit.

payload.description: Charts and tables must use a uniform 12px rounded corner radius on all four corners, rather than the asymmetric brand accent shape.

description: Candidate rules completely omit rules for chart container styling, color application logic, rounded bar shapes, and chart headlines, leaving critical design specifications uncaptured.

rules.description: Charts must use uniform rounded corners and a Light Blue (#E6F0F9) panel with a Brand Blue rounded header bar. Bars and chart icons must feature rounded, approachable shapes like rounded bar tops.

Chart color logic must feature Brand Blue (#00529b) and Sky Blue (#358CCB) as dominant colors, while Sunshine (#faa31b) and Gold (#ffc60a) are reserved strictly for emphasis on key figures/data points, with neutral gray for comparator elements.
Chart headlines must use ALL CAPS in Agenda for print or Nunito Sans Bold+ for email, styled in Brand Blue with a thin Gold rule beneath.

description: The rule for statistical callouts has 'must' polarity but has 'strong_default' hardness without defining the conditional governance or scope for when deviations from the hero percentage rule are permitted.

payload.description: Statistical callouts must be large and bold with the percentage figure serving as the hero element, and supporting copy styled in a lighter weight directly beneath.

description: The candidate rules for this brand blob are empty, causing a complete failure to extract three distinct iconography design rules defined in the source units.

rules.description: Icons must use a minimal line illustration style featuring consistent stroke weight, rounded line caps, and single-color line work.
Icon colors and backgrounds must only be adjusted within the approved brand palette.
Icons must maintain high contrast for low-vision audiences, with a minimum render size of 32x32px in emails, utilizing default Brand Blue strokes on light backgrounds.

description: The candidate rule list has missed several critical rules defined in the layout principles source units, including focal point limits and text-wall reduction principles.

payload.rule_name: Layout White Space and Focal Points
payload.rule_text: Maintain generous white space to avoid clutter, and limit focal points per section by using contrast to direct attention to the single most important element.

description: The requirement for designers to declare their section background color in component metadata to prevent adjacent background collision is missing from candidate rules.

payload.rule_text: Section designers must declare their section's background color in the component metadata so assemblers can sequence without two identical adjacent backgrounds.

description: Missing reference template rule. The patient brochure is defined as the canonical reference template for all Lisraya materials.

payload.rule_text: Use the patient brochure (bold headline + simple benefit statements + patient quote) as the canonical content block reference template for visuals, messaging, and overall tone of all Lisraya materials.

description: The candidate rules cluster completely misses the brand typography rules for 'Agenda' and 'Nunito Sans', as well as the geometric CTA shape configuration containing specific gradient stops. These cannot be cleanly combined into a color hierarchy rule and need distinct rule nodes.
Scope mismatch for Nunito Sans font rule: it is explicitly limited to 'print' applications rather than all media.

The entire Accessibility section is completely unmapped to any candidate rules. These must be parsed and extracted into distinct logical brand rules matching their distinct subjects (Typography, Form Controls, Animations/Email GIFs, and General Media standards).

rules.rule_text: Ensure baseline accessibility for typography and hierarchy: body copy must be 16px or larger, line height must be 150% or higher (especially for light-on-dark text), follow a structured type scale, test contrast, and verify legibility without relying strictly on color.
For forms, keep labels visible at all times (avoiding placeholder-only labels or disappearing text labels), and maintain a minimum click/tap target size of 48px.

Keep UI animations quick, unobtrusive, and simple with responsive layouts. For email formats, animated GIFs must deliver their core message in the first frame (to accommodate systems like Outlook), and autoplaying motion must not exceed 5 seconds without offering a static fallback.
Provide descriptive alt text for all images and make transcripts available for video or audio assets to meet global WCAG reference standards.

description: The candidate rule captures the terminology constraints but misses the explicit governance mechanism mandated in the source: 'Confirm audience in every brief before writing'.

payload.governance_facet: Confirm target audience in every creative brief before writing.

description: Critical assembly-level formatting, color coding, footnote handling, and adjacent background restrictions are completely missing candidate rule mappings, risking lost governance rules.

payload.description: Enforce hexadecimal colors, pixel sizing, inline CSS with Outlook bgcolor duplication, superscript placeholder footnote tokens, and non-adjacent placement of dark-background sections.
